arts.ttl: England-Ireland lineups confirmed as Harry Kane returns and debutant in defence
arts.sum: England captain Harry Kane returns to the Three Lions' starting lineup for their UEFA Nations League clash with the Republic of Ireland at Wembley.

arts.ttl: England name team to face Ireland – Walker in at centre-back
arts.sum: Lee Carsley has named his England team to face the Republic of Ireland at Wembley this evening. The interim manager takes charge of England for the final time tonight and has made two changes from the 3-0 win in Greece on Thursday night. Valentino Livramento makes his England debut at right-back in place of the […] The post England name team to face Ireland – Walker in at centre-back first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: England v Republic of Ireland: Nations League – live
arts.sum: Minute-by-minute updates on the action at Wembley Any comments or thoughts? You can email Rob Lee Carsley’s spell as interim England manager has been like a trip to the dentist. Okay, it’s had enough in common for a half-arsed analogy. We spent much of the time wincing, whining and worrying, only to belatedly realise it’s less painful than we feared and we’re in a healthier state than we were beforehand. If England beat the Republic of Ireland at Wembley this evening, they will win Group B2 and return to – ludicrous phrase alert – the Nations League top table. In fact all they need to do is match Greece’s result in Finland, so victory may not be essential. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: England v Ireland LIVE: Nations League team news and line-ups as England make three changes at Wembley
arts.sum: England can earn promotion in the Nations League with a victory at Wembley, in Lee Carsley’s final game

arts.ttl: Postcard from London: Folly to write off Ireland?
arts.sum: And the Ireland team arrived at the venue late on Saturday evening for a pitch walk having trained in Dublin in the morning before taking flight to London for their final UEFA Nations League game, which wraps up the 2024 calendar. But for football fans, the stadium was all about one Saturday every May when the FA Cup final would dominate the airwaves, as television viewers followed the two teams from breakfast at the hotel, on the coach to the ground and all the way out the tunnel onto the hallowed turf, stopping for an always rousing edition of Abide With Me just before kick-off.

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer turns up at football match in disguise after pulling out of England duty
